que: What is the primary purpose of a Kotlin enum class?
options: Represent a fixed set of type-safe constants

que: What is each enum constant in Kotlin?
options: An object instance of the enum class

que: Which API lists enum entries in modern Kotlin?
What does `valueOf()` do for an enum class?
options: Gets an enum constant by name

que: What happens if `valueOf()` receives a bad name?
Which property gives the declared position of an enum constant?
Why should you be careful with `ordinal`?
options: It depends on declaration order and can change if entries are reordered
It cannot be read at runtime

que: Can enum entries have constructor parameters or methods?
When is an enum a good modeling choice?
options: When the valid values form a known, closed set
When values change from a server every minute
When you need arbitrary user-generated strings
